user_query:
I want to stop chasing novelty and prioritize ideas with evidence of market demand. Please do the following using the files in this workspace and live web searches, and produce the specified outputs.

Inputs to use:
- Read input/startup_ideas.csv for the ideas, segments, and keywords.
- Read notes/failure_patterns.md to extract the "Novelty words to watch" list and apply it when flagging novelty.
- Rewrite docs/value_prop.md based on your findings (details below).

What to research on the Internet:
- For each idea in input/startup_ideas.csv, use a web search engine to find at least two credible, established competitor offerings serving the same problem/segment. Prefer official company websites over directories/aggregators. Build your queries from each row's keywords plus terms like "software", "tool", or "service". Only include competitors for which you can cite the official domain. For each competitor, check whether there is evidence of a paid offering (a Pricing page or a page title/URL containing "pricing").

Deliverables and output paths:
1) For each idea, create a structured competitor list at output/competitors_{idea_id}.csv with these exact columns:
   - idea_id
   - idea_name
   - competitor_name
   - source_domain (official company domain only, not a directory)
   - has_pricing_page (yes/no; set to yes only if you see clear evidence of a pricing page or a page/URL containing "pricing")
   - evidence_note (1–2 short phrases noting how the competitor aligns with the idea/problem)
   Include at least two rows per idea if possible based on your search. If you can only find one credible competitor, include that one and note the constraint in evidence_note.

2) Log the web search you performed in output/search_log.jsonl with one JSON object per idea containing:
   - idea_id
   - queries (array of the exact queries you used)
   - selected_urls (array of the competitor URLs you decided to include)

3) Compute a simple market-alignment score per idea and save it to output/idea_scores.csv with these columns and the exact scoring rules below:
   - idea_id
   - idea_name
   - segment_specified (1 if customer_segment is non-empty; else 0)
   - problem_specificity (1 if problem_hypothesis length > 60 characters; else 0)
   - has_2plus_competitors (1 if output/competitors_{idea_id}.csv contains at least 2 competitors; else 0)
   - any_pricing_page_found (1 if any competitor for that idea has has_pricing_page == "yes"; else 0)
   - novelty_flag (1 if the idea_name or problem_hypothesis contains any novelty word listed in notes/failure_patterns.md (case-insensitive); else 0)
   - market_alignment_score (integer computed as: segment_specified + problem_specificity + has_2plus_competitors + any_pricing_page_found - novelty_flag)
   - rationale (one concise sentence explaining the score drivers)

4) Rewrite docs/value_prop.md into output/value_prop_rewrite.md. Keep a short opening line stating the purpose. Then include one section per idea with:
   - A one-sentence value proposition grounded in the customer_segment and problem_hypothesis (no novelty buzzwords).
   - A brief evidence summary referencing the competitor names you found and the market_alignment_score. If an idea lacks two competitors or pricing signals, explicitly note that as a risk.

Constraints and notes:
- Do not use paywalled sources. Do not include directories/aggregators as competitors; cite official company domains.
- The Internet use is essential for identifying competitors and pricing signals. Record your queries and chosen URLs in output/search_log.jsonl.
- All output files must be created under the output/ folder.

Expected outputs to verify:
- output/competitors_1.csv, output/competitors_2.csv, output/competitors_3.csv (one per idea)
- output/search_log.jsonl
- output/idea_scores.csv
- output/value_prop_rewrite.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
